2606.20093.title: Self-Preference Is Weak or Absent in Verifiable Instruction-Following Revision: A Four-Model Test Under Genuine Authorship
2606.20093.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly review and revise text, including their own. A documented self-preference bias (models favoring their own generations when acting as judges) raises the question of whether models also resist valid corrections to their own writing. We test this in a setting where "valid" is decided not by another model but by a deterministic verifier: instruction-following revision on IFEval. A model writes a draft; the official IFEval checker confirms the draft violates a constraint and that a candidate edit fixes it; the model then accepts or rejects that edit either as the genuine in-context author or as a fresh model that sees the draft neutrally. Across four mid-tier model families and 85 author-versus-fresh comparisons, we find no detectable self-preference: authors reject verified-good fixes to their own drafts at essentially the same rate as fresh models judging the same drafts (gap -5.1 pp, 95% CI [-12.9, +2.7]). A self-skepticism hint from a smaller pilot did not replicate at scale. The one robust observation is qualitative: when authors do reject a verified-good fix, 97% of their stated reasons are flaw-catching rather than preference, that is, about the character of rejections, not an elevated rate. Effects smaller than ~13 pp cannot be excluded at this sample size.
2606.20093.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 日益檢視和修訂文本，包括它們自己的文本。已記錄的自我偏好偏見（模型在擔任評審時偏愛自己的生成內容）引發了模型是否也會抵制對其自身寫作的有效修正的問題。我們在一個設定中測試這一點，其中「有效」不是由另一個模型決定，而是由一個確定性驗證者決定：在 IFEval 上進行的遵循指令的修訂。一個模型撰寫草稿；官方 IFEval 檢查器確認該草稿違反了一個約束，並且一個候選編輯修正了它；然後模型作為真正的上下文作者或作為一個中立地看待草稿的新模型接受或拒絕該編輯。在四個中等規模的模型系列和 85 次作者對新模型的比較中，我們發現沒有可檢測的自我偏好：作者拒絕經過驗證的良好修正的比率基本上與新模型評判相同的草稿的比率相同（差距 -5.1 pp，95% CI [-12.9, +2.7]）。來自一個較小的試點的自我懷疑暗示在大規模上未能重現。唯一的穩健觀察是質性的：當作者拒絕經過驗證的良好修正時，他們所陳述的理由中有 97% 是關於缺陷捕捉而非偏好，即關於拒絕的性質，而不是提高的比率。在這個樣本大小下，無法排除小於 ~13 pp 的效應。
2606.20093.author: William Guey et.al.
